workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Angela Doyle at Contoso Ltd just sent this. Help me investigate the decline.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 13:01 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Contoso Ltd

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 10:15 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 17:56 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-M trap (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-M failure mode (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-M -- Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
